### Post by NickL87 on 2011-07-25
Hi,

I am in need of sorting the content of a csv-file separated with "#". The file looks the following:

```

Descriptive 1#47.5498295127948#35.5365757501888#44.4127802931231#84.0767444257134#0.0416435951243528#0.018723884762542
Descriptive 2#76.04903049509#69.9412849890004#81.816974052306#83.9049496373567#0.0535382310892861#0.0188892659268238
Descriptive 3#84.7545616236762#81.3084027771508#92.2867743752168#85.4583971996571#0.0775541043151416#0.0190649249280866
Directional#95.0617395412946#93.68490112344#7.76099711389795#62.3496365149169#0.0322573681203777#0.0339278121725953
Dynamic#95.5527747188574#94.8249741382187#78.1754016815084#1214.29940747158#0.446743303244314#0.0351562900446475
Non-directional#93.7739818167044#93.2560498129607#97.9310151912019#101.501494431925#0.139673075841138#0.0193306886805814
Predictive 1#93.7800007824656#92.0242684699488#3.90006937060051#55.586085493858#0.0312529364434865#0.0350568216769591
Predictive 2#93.7721761269761#92.101010283403#12.3741496344998#87.1585923857413#0.0334431207295733#0.0534945213017153

Profiling 10#94.6009877122814#92.9274142824039#11.7273260665561#159.990712146313#0.0354011566623827#0.0680905486516897

```

I sort it using the following bash command in the terminal:

```

sort -t "#" -k5 csv.dat

```

and get the following result:

```

Non-directional#93.7739818167044#93.2560498129607#97.9310151912019#101.501494431925#0.139673075841138#0.0193306886805814

Profiling 10#94.6009877122814#92.9274142824039#11.7273260665561#159.990712146313#0.0354011566623827#0.0680905486516897
Predictive 1#93.7800007824656#92.0242684699488#3.90006937060051#55.586085493858#0.0312529364434865#0.0350568216769591
Directional#95.0617395412946#93.68490112344#7.76099711389795#62.3496365149169#0.0322573681203777#0.0339278121725953
Descriptive 2#76.04903049509#69.9412849890004#81.816974052306#83.9049496373567#0.0535382310892861#0.0188892659268238

Predictive 2#93.7721761269761#92.101010283403#12.3741496344998#87.1585923857413#0.0334431207295733#0.0534945213017153

```

The data, however, does not seem to be sorted correctly for column 5. 

Any ideas?

### Post by Wayne_V on 2011-07-25
it works for me:


> $ cat csv.dat  | sort -t '#' -k5  -n | tr "#" "\t"
Predictive 1	93.7800007824656	92.0242684699488	3.90006937060051	55.586085493858	0.0312529364434865	0.0350568216769591
Directional	95.0617395412946	93.68490112344	7.76099711389795	62.3496365149169	0.0322573681203777	0.0339278121725953
Descriptive 2	76.04903049509	69.9412849890004	81.816974052306	83.9049496373567	0.0535382310892861	0.0188892659268238
Descriptive 1	47.5498295127948	35.5365757501888	44.4127802931231	84.0767444257134	0.0416435951243528	0.018723884762542
Descriptive 3	84.7545616236762	81.3084027771508	92.2867743752168	85.4583971996571	0.0775541043151416	0.0190649249280866
Predictive 2	93.7721761269761	92.101010283403	12.3741496344998	87.1585923857413	0.0334431207295733	0.0534945213017153
Non-directional	93.7739818167044	93.2560498129607	97.9310151912019	101.501494431925	0.139673075841138	0.0193306886805814
Predictive 3	93.8492188887184	92.2277095126745	16.9588006429792	119.652912929239	0.0348211238037506	0.0704881327952719
Profiling 10	94.6009877122814	92.9274142824039	11.7273260665561	159.990712146313	0.0354011566623827	0.0680905486516897

### Post by NickL87 on 2011-07-25
> **blind2314 said:**
> I'm confused as to how that's actually sorted..it doesn't seem to be in either ascending or descending order, left to right or bottom to top.

If you look at column 5 you will see that it is sorted in descending order :-)

### Post by NickL87 on 2011-07-25
No one who can help?

---

### Post by NickL87 on 2011-07-25
Problem solved.

It is a language problem. I am sitting in Denmark where the delimeter is different from ".", giving that floating point does not work as it should, so I changed the environment variable to LANG=C.

---
